key_insight: A robust two-part pour-casting workflow and sensor-embedding procedure can make soft pneumatic actuator fabrication repeatable, scalable, and air-tight.
engineering_relevance: The paper provides a practical manufacturing and validation pipeline for soft robotic actuators, combining FEM, controlled actuation, and sensor calibration to support reliable deployment.
method: Two-part silicone pour casting with steps to prevent cavity clogging and improve sealing
Thin-film flex sensor embedding with repeatable placement for bending measurement
FEM stress/deformation analysis plus PID-controlled pneumatic tests and image-based calibration
evidence_signal: The methods were validated across two operators with 24 successful fabrications, and simulated bending angles showed comparable response to experimental measurements.
watch_item: Performance is still influenced by viscoelastic hysteresis and careful manual execution, so consistency may depend on operator discipline and fabrication quality control.
paper_title: Robust Silicone Pour Casting and Sensor Embedding Procedures for Soft Robotic Actuators
